requirements.title: Preserve store-only runtime recall
requirements.description: Runtime retrieval must continue reading canonical knowledge from resolved mounted stores rather than project-local .fabric/knowledge roots.

requirements.title: Keep fab_recall as default path
requirements.description: One-step recall remains the default user-facing retrieval flow; two-step plan/fetch is for oversized context or manual selection.

requirements.title: Make vector retrieval observable
requirements.description: Operators need proof whether semantic search is actually active or silently falling back to text-only.

constraints.constraint: Knowledge search MUST preserve store-qualified ids and provenance.
constraints.rationale: Local ids can shadow across stores.

constraints.constraint: Normal retrieval MUST NOT inject pending drafts.
constraints.rationale: Pending entries are review-only in the architecture contract.

constraints.constraint: Vector semantic search MUST remain supplementary to BM25, not override lexical relevance.
Semantic search MUST stay opt-in and reversible by setting embed_enabled=false.
constraints.rationale: Optional dependency and CPU/model footprint should not affect default installs.

domain.problem_statement: Knowledge search in Fabric spans store-backed runtime recall, two-step candidate/body retrieval, and review/admin substring search. The main runtime path is mostly store-only aligned, but performance, explicit-id semantics, semantic-search observability, and surface-alignment gaps remain.

terminology.definition: Umbrella for runtime recall, two-step retrieval, and review search.
Knowledge source of truth is mounted stores' knowledge tree.
Optional dense vector scoring via fastembed.

domain.audience: Fabric maintainers and AI tooling operators

non_goals.title: Implement code changes in this grill
non_goals.rationale: This session is an architectural stress test and evidence package.

insights.summary: Main runtime recall is store-only aligned, but schemas/docs/comments still expose retired local paths.
Config enables vectors in this repo, but runtime activation requires optional fastembed import success.
The largest remaining bottleneck is O(N) store walking and file reads, not just scoring.

open_questions.question: Should fab_recall ids bypass top_k and fetch directly from store body index?
Should Fabric persist a store-local derived search index, and what invalidates it?
Should doc vector cache be keyed by model as well as text?
